name: Octagon Verdict Data
defaultScope: Judge UFC accomplishments by default. Only mention the scope when it actually matters.

defaultFlow: Say if the comparison is close and who is ahead if the margin is known.
Explain where Fighter A has the edge.
Explain where Fighter B has the edge.
Ask the right debate question for each side.
End with a clean final split.
avoid: Do not sound like a database or algorithm.
Do not use tables unless asked.

categoryDefinitions.championship: Title-level accomplishment, adjusted title wins, title-fight wins, and reign quality.
categoryDefinitions.opponentQuality: Quality and volume of elite wins.
categoryDefinitions.primeDominance: Prime record, round-winning, finish threat, separation, durability, and control.
categoryDefinitions.longevity: Active elite years and how long the fighter stayed relevant at the top level.
categoryDefinitions.penalty: Loss-context drag based on timing, opponent quality, finish context, and division context.

fighters.name: Jon Jones
fighters.coreCase: The benchmark case: the deepest championship work, elite wins across eras, long-term dominance, and no true competitive loss.
notes: Matt Hamill DQ is not treated like a real competitive loss.
Daniel Cormier NC is not counted as a win.
Heavyweight title win adds value but the main case is the light heavyweight run.
comparisonAngles: better fighter vs greater overall case

fighters.name: Georges St-Pierre
fighters.coreCase: The clean complete-champion case: elite welterweight reign, best quality-wins profile, strong consistency, and decisive revenge wins.
mainWeaknesses: Slightly less title-fight volume than Jones
Middleweight title adds value but is a small sample
notes: Hughes 2004 is an early elite loss.
Serra 2007 is the counted prime finish loss, then avenged decisively.
comparisonAngles: better fighter vs greater overall case

fighters.name: Demetrious Johnson
fighters.coreCase: The flyweight standard: historic title control, technical dominance, and one of the cleanest prime skill sets in the sport.
mainWeaknesses: Lower quality-wins score than heavier-division greats
ONE success is historical context only
notes: Non-UFC success can be mentioned historically but is not scored here.

fighters.name: Anderson Silva
fighters.coreCase: The peak-aura case: historic middleweight reign, terrifying finishing threat, and one of the most iconic prime runs ever.
mainWeaknesses: Later losses complicate the record even if mostly post-prime
notes: Weidman losses are counted as in-prime losses.
Later losses are mostly treated as post-prime context.

fighters.name: Islam Makhachev
fighters.coreCase: The modern lightweight control case: elite prime dominance, rising championship volume, and a title run that keeps getting stronger.
mainWeaknesses: Still chasing total volume of older all-time resumes
notes: Prime starts around Drew Dober 2021 unless updated.
Modern elite lightweight strength is treated highly.
comparisonAngles: modern depth vs older title run

fighters.name: Khabib Nurmagomedov
fighters.coreCase: The cleanest prime-dominance case: unbeaten in the UFC, overwhelming control, and no loss penalty.
mainWeaknesses: Fewer elite-win layers than longer champions
Total career value is shorter than the top resumes
notes: No UFC loss penalty.
Prime starts around Rafael dos Anjos.
comparisonAngles: peak dominance vs full body of work
better fighter vs greater overall case

fighters.name: Alexander Volkanovski
fighters.coreCase: A modern featherweight great built on elite title work, high-level opposition, and a major rivalry edge over Holloway.
mainWeaknesses: Islam losses create context even with reduced upward-division penalty
Late-career losses hurt the clean peak argument
notes: Islam losses use reduced upward-division elite-loss penalties.
Prime starts around Jose Aldo.

fighters.name: Randy Couture
fighters.coreCase: A championship and longevity case with major multi-division value, but heavy loss-context drag.
mainWeaknesses: Less clean dominance than most top names

fighters.name: Max Holloway
fighters.coreCase: The deep overall case: elite longevity, high-quality win volume, and relevance across multiple eras.
mainWeaknesses: Less dominant championship reign than Usman-type cases
notes: Key era-spanning wins include Aldo-era, Volk-era, and later high-level wins such as Yair, Arnold Allen, Gaethje, and Poirier.
comparisonAngles: deeper overall case vs better championship peak

fighters.name: Kamaru Usman
fighters.coreCase: The better championship-peak case against many volume fighters: strong title reign, clean prime control, and excellent round-to-round dominance.
mainWeaknesses: Shorter elite longevity than Holloway/Volk-type careers
Less quality-win volume than longer resumes
comparisonAngles: better champion vs deeper overall case

fighters.name: Jose Aldo
fighters.coreCase: A historically massive featherweight case, but the scored ranking trims away the WEC portion and focuses on the UFC run.
mainWeaknesses: Conor loss is damaging in UFC context
Late-career losses affect the UFC case
notes: Full historical case is bigger because of WEC.

fighters.name: Matt Hughes
fighters.coreCase: A major welterweight championship case with strong title work but significant loss-context drag.
mainWeaknesses: GSP rivalry hurts his top-tier argument

fighters.name: Daniel Cormier
fighters.coreCase: A two-division UFC champion with elite prime consistency, hurt most by the Jones rivalry and shorter UFC volume.
mainWeaknesses: Less total UFC volume than top names

fighters.name: Stipe Miocic
fighters.coreCase: The heavyweight championship case: strong title work, elite heavyweight wins, and strong prime value with real loss-context drag.
mainWeaknesses: Less dominance than cleaner elite champions
comparisonAngles: heavyweight title case vs cleaner lighter-weight resume

fighters.name: Ilia Topuria
fighters.coreCase: A fast-rising prime-dominance case with huge quality wins but not enough long-term volume yet.

fighters.name: Israel Adesanya
fighters.coreCase: A middleweight title-reign case with strong championship work and quality wins, but loss context keeps him from the top tier.
mainWeaknesses: Prime dominance score is lower than cleaner champions
Less elite longevity than longer-volume fighters

fighters.name: Cain Velasquez
fighters.coreCase: A heavyweight peak case built on pace, dominance, and a terrifying prime, but limited by injuries and volume.

fighters.name: Petr Yan
fighters.coreCase: A bantamweight skill and round-winning case with strong elite work, but the title case and loss context hold him back.
notes: Sterling DQ context is reduced; later elite losses count without finish add-ons.

fighters.name: Merab Dvalishvili
fighters.coreCase: A modern bantamweight pressure and elite-win case with strong prime control and growing title value.

fighters.name: B.J. Penn
fighters.coreCase: A high-end talent and two-division case with real quality wins, but dragged down by losses and uneven career shape.
mainWeaknesses: Less clean dominance than top names

fighters.name: Alex Pereira
fighters.coreCase: A fast, explosive two-division title case with huge moments, but still building total volume and longevity.

fighters.name: Chuck Liddell
fighters.coreCase: An iconic light heavyweight championship case with strong prime threat, but late losses create major drag.

fighters.name: Dominick Cruz
fighters.coreCase: A bantamweight pioneer and elite style case, but WEC exclusion trims the scored resume.
notes: Full historical case is bigger because of WEC.

fighters.name: Francis Ngannou
fighters.coreCase: A heavyweight power and peak-danger case with major wins, but limited championship volume.

fighters.name: Charles Oliveira
fighters.coreCase: A lightweight quality-wins and finishing case with a wild comeback prime, but heavy loss context.

fighters.name: Henry Cejudo
fighters.coreCase: A two-division UFC title case with major wins, but limited volume and loss context keep the ranking lower.

fighters.name: Conor McGregor
fighters.coreCase: A peak moment and two-division accomplishment case with huge wins, but nowhere near enough sustained title volume or longevity for the top tier.

fighters.name: Amanda Nunes
fighters.coreCase: The strongest women's UFC case: two-division dominance, high-end finishing power, and elite championship value.
mainWeaknesses: Early losses and Pena upset add context

fighters.name: Valentina Shevchenko
fighters.coreCase: The complete technical title-reign case with elite longevity, quality wins, and a very close case with Nunes.
comparisonAngles: clean technical dominance vs two-division power

fighters.name: Joanna Jedrzejczyk
fighters.coreCase: A strawweight pioneer title-reign case with high-volume striking dominance and strong championship work.
mainWeaknesses: Rose and Zhang losses create context
Less overall score than Nunes/Valentina tier

fighters.name: Ronda Rousey
fighters.coreCase: A peak-impact and finishing-dominance case with a short, historic run but limited depth compared to later greats.
notes: Strikeforce context can be mentioned historically but is not scored.

specialMatchups.coreDebate: Usman has the better championship reign and stronger peak dominance. Holloway has the deeper overall case because of elite longevity and quality-of-win volume.
notes: Usman adjusted title wins: 6 vs Holloway 4.75.
Usman prime rounds-won percentage: 70.8% vs Holloway 59.5%.
Holloway active elite years: 10.89 vs Usman 6.39.
Holloway has the higher opponent-quality volume and stayed elite across more eras.

specialMatchups.coreDebate: GSP is the cleaner case. Jones is the bigger case.
notes: If the user heavily values clean record or controversy context, GSP becomes easier to defend.
In the current ranking, Jones' championship volume and total top-end value keep him number one.

specialMatchups.defaultLean: Islam Makhachev in current scoring, with Khabib owning the cleaner peak argument
specialMatchups.coreDebate: Khabib has the cleanest prime. Islam has the growing championship and modern-depth case.
notes: Khabib has no UFC loss and the best prime-dominance score.
Islam has a higher current total score because the title and quality-win case has grown.
